textPlain:
RSA may well be be deprecated long before quantum computing can break it, simply as a precautionary measure. Post-quantum cryptography standards are already being deployed:
GnuPG 2.5 and upcoming 2.6 have introduced support for Kyber (ML-KEM, FIPS-203).
Kyber is added in in OpenSSH 9.9 (September 2024) as a hybrid post-quantum key exchange algorithm and made the default key exchange mechanism in OpenSSH 10.0 (April 2025). The implementation uses a hybrid approach, combining classical cryptography (X25519) with ML-KEM-768 for key exchange (mlkem768x25519-sha256). 
OpenSSH plans to add support for post-quantum signature algorithms in the future.
https://medium.com/asecuritysite-when-bob-met-alice/a-long-g...The important and missing context that hasn't been mentioned by John is why progress has been slow: decoherence and error rates. Shor's algorithm requires fault-tolerant quantum computing, which didn't exist in any form until recently.
The key is that the physical error rate must be lower than the threshold required. Different research groups and companies, using various qubit technologies demonstrated techniques and elements of below-threshold error correction. 
https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-08449-y
